# Identity and access management

Vault Radar access is governed through HashiCorp Cloud Platform (HCP) identity and access management. Operators assign organization-level roles to teams — see [People and process](/validated-designs/vault-radar/administration-guide/people-and-process) for the recommended role per stakeholder — and then use resource-based access control to grant least-privileged access to specific data sources.

<!-- EDITORIAL REWRITE (HVD 2.0 migration): resource-based access control content relocated here from the source Remediation operating-guide page, because granting and managing access is operator (Administration) work per spec §8.4. The consumer-facing remediation workflow remains in the User Guide. Please review. -->

## Resource-based access control

Vault Radar administrators can grant developer access to specific resources for least-privileged access at the group level.

![Resource-based access control configuration](/img/vault-radar/operating-guides/remediation-2.png)

Create groups at the HCP organization level, and then assign them to one or more data sources or repositories with Resource Contributor or Viewer roles. While the Resource Viewer role can only view information about a risk, a Resource Contributor can view and modify the state of a risk, possibly applying remediation to it.

![Group and role assignment interface](/img/vault-radar/operating-guides/remediation-3.png)

For more on HCP roles and access management, see the [HCP access management documentation](https://developer.hashicorp.com/hcp/docs/hcp/iam/access-management).
